text:
"YOU DON'T GIVE UP EASILY,
DO YOU?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "OH!  YOU AGAIN!"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "STAND FAST!"
speaker: gargoyle

text:
"CAN YOU HEAR ME?
YOU HAVE THREE QUESTIONS."
speaker: gargoyle

text: "HERE COME YOUR QUESTIONS:"
speaker: gargoyle

text:
"HE WHO WOULD THE WIZARD SEE
FIRST MUST ANSWER QUESTIONS THREE."
speaker: gargoyle

text: "BEAT IT, BUB!"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WRONG!"
speaker: gargoyle

text:
ASKING QUESTIONS
IS *MY* JOB, BUB!
speaker: gargoyle

text: "NO THIEVES ALLOWED!"
speaker: gargoyle

text: The Wizard is not running an inn.
speaker: narrator

text: "THAT'S NOT NICE!"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WHAT IS YOUR NAME?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WHAT IS YOUR FAVORITE COLOR?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WHAT IS YOUR QUEST?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WHO DO YOU SEEK HERE?"
speaker: gargoyle

text:
"WHAT IS THE MEAN AIRSPEED
OF AN UNLADEN SWALLOW?"
speaker: gargoyle

text:
"WHAT IS THE MEANING OF LIFE,
THE UNIVERSE, AND EVERYTHING?"
speaker: gargoyle

text:
"IS IT FURTHER TO SHAPEIR,
OR BY CAMEL?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "IS THIS A YES OR NO QUESTION?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WHAT IS THE BARON'S FIRST NAME?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WHOSE SPELL PROTECTS THE TOWN?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "WHAT IS THE THIEVES' PASSWORD?"
speaker: gargoyle

text: "GOOD.  YOU'RE NOT A THIEF!"
speaker: gargoyle

text: THAT IS CORRECT.
speaker: gargoyle

text: THE WIZARD WILL NOW SEE YOU.
speaker: gargoyle

text:
GO DIRECTLY UP TO THE TOWER.
DO NOT DALLY!
speaker: gargoyle

text: There seems to be a fox north of the road.
speaker: narrator

text: The forest deepens north and south of you.  To the east, the road twists it's way up the mountain.  To the west, the road is hidden by trees.
speaker: narrator

text: For a moment there, you thought you saw something. You can't see anything now.
speaker: narrator

text: It seems to be a simple spring trap and you can easily open it.
speaker: narrator

text: The trap seems to be caught on something.
speaker: narrator

text: You can see no sign of a trap here.
speaker: narrator

text: The fox seems to have a foot caught in a trap.
speaker: narrator

text: The fox is gone.
speaker: narrator

text: You must free the fox first.
speaker: narrator

text: Walk over to the trap.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see the trap now.  It seems to have vanished.
speaker: narrator

text: There is an aura of magic around the fox.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no magic present.
speaker: narrator

text: The fox is in such pain that it's already dazzled.
speaker: narrator

text: "You don't have to put me out of my misery, you just have to get me out of this trap."
speaker: fox

text: "You realize, of course, I really find it hard to relax with this thing on my leg."
speaker: fox

text: "Help me, Brave and Kind Hero."
speaker: fox

text: "Please help me, Brave and Kind Hero."
speaker: fox

text: "Please have mercy on a creature who is in great pain."
speaker: fox

text: The fox does not seem to want to fight.
speaker: narrator

text: "My foot is caught in this cruel trap and I am in great pain.  Surely you could take a minute and set me free."
speaker: fox

text: That was strange, no sooner did you kill the fox than it vanishes.
speaker: narrator

text: Hmmm.  Perhaps it needed a stronger spell.
speaker: narrator

text: Your powerful Open spell springs the trap.
speaker: narrator

text: You spring the trap.
speaker: narrator

text: "In exchange for your kindness, I will give you some advice and a bit of information.  First of all, it sometimes pays off to be polite, even to rude people."
speaker: fox

text: "As for the amusing tidbit - Baba Yaga put an enchantment on the Baron's Daughter some years back.  To break the spell you need to talk to the Dryad.  Au Revoir, Ta-ta, see you sometime."
speaker: fox

text: The trap seems to be caught on something.  You can't pull it loose.
speaker: narrator

text: "I am in such pain and agony I cannot think.   Help me, good adventurer, please."
speaker: fox

text: That's not worth any game points, and will not make the fox feel any better.
speaker: narrator

text: "My foot is caught in this trap.  If I had hands like you, instead of paws, I could free myself."
speaker: fox

text: Walk around to the other side of the fox, where the trap is.
speaker: narrator

text: There's no one here to fight.
speaker: narrator

text: You would need a sword.
speaker: narrator

text: Ten silver coins equal a gold coin in value.
speaker: narrator

text: Each gold coin is worth 10 silver coins.
speaker: narrator

text: Standard adventuring rations.
speaker: narrator

text: Root of a Mandrake plant.
speaker: narrator

text: A large, metallic key.
speaker: narrator

text: A heavy, one-handed broadsword.
speaker: narrator

text: Daggers for throwing and close-in fighting.
speaker: narrator

text: Armor made of leather boiled in wax.
speaker: narrator

text: A strong (and heavy) iron shield.
speaker: narrator

text: Blank pieces of paper.
speaker: narrator

text: Small apples from last Fall's harvest.
speaker: narrator

text: Vegetables from the Centaur farmers.
speaker: narrator

text: This gem glows with a magic of its own.
speaker: narrator

text: A lovely alabaster flower vase.
speaker: narrator

text: A heavy golden candelabra.
speaker: narrator

text: The gaudily-ornamented music box plays "Fur Elise".
speaker: narrator

text: A finely-made silver candlestick
speaker: narrator

text: A string of pearls
speaker: narrator

text: A fine golden ring.
speaker: narrator

text: Seed of the Spore-Spitting Spirea.
speaker: narrator

text: Small rocks, suitable for throwing.
speaker: narrator

text: A flask of beautiful flowers.
speaker: narrator

text: An Acme Lock Pick.
speaker: narrator

text: Tool kit with several types of lockpicks.
speaker: narrator

text: Your Thieves' Guild License.
speaker: narrator

text: An empty flask with a cork stopper.
speaker: narrator

text: Soft green Meep fur.
speaker: narrator

text: A flask of genuine Fairy Dust.
speaker: narrator

text: Crystal clear water in a glass flask.
speaker: narrator

text: Magic mushrooms.  Or maybe toadstools.
speaker: narrator

text: Claws from a Cheetah-Centaur.
speaker: narrator

text: Troll beard with hairs as tough as wire.
speaker: narrator

text: Chainmail Armor (*very* heavy!)
speaker: narrator

text: A potion to restore Health Points.
speaker: narrator

text: A potion to restore Magic Points.
speaker: narrator

text: A potion to restore Stamina Points.
speaker: narrator

text: A potion for dispelling enchantments.
speaker: narrator

text: An oil to protect you from the Living Dead.
speaker: narrator

text: A mirror with a face (not yours) on it.
speaker: narrator

text: Acorn from the Dryad's tree.
speaker: narrator

text: You're kidding, right?
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have a knife.
speaker: narrator

text: There are none here to search.
speaker: narrator

text: The Ogre's body must have been carried away during the night by some marauding beasties.
speaker: narrator

text: However, there is a dead Ogre here.
speaker: narrator

text: However, there is a very large Ogre here.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a cave entrance large enough to admit a man.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a big, ugly, dead Ogre here.
speaker: narrator

text: This Ogre is in a bad mood.
speaker: narrator

text: And big. Very BIG.
speaker: narrator

text: The dead Ogre's chest lies open and empty.
speaker: narrator

text: The dead Ogre's chest lies open, its contents glittering brightly.
speaker: narrator

text: The Ogre is carrying a chest in his massive arms.
speaker: narrator

text: The chest has a heavy lid with a strong lock.
speaker: narrator

text: There is nobody here.
speaker: narrator

text: This Ogre will never eat again.
speaker: narrator

text: This Ogre looks like it would rather eat you.
speaker: narrator

text: There are no locks here.
speaker: narrator

text: The chest is already open.
speaker: narrator

text: It's locked -- you'll have to use another skill to open this.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have the right tools -- you'll have to find another way to open it.
speaker: narrator

text: The lock is beyond your current skill.
speaker: narrator

text: There's nothing like that here.
speaker: narrator

text: It's already unlocked.
speaker: narrator

text: A magical aura emanates from the cave's entrance.
speaker: narrator

text: You've wasted a spell.
speaker: narrator

text: Ok, he'll be a dazzled cadaver.
speaker: narrator

text: How much more calm can he get?
speaker: narrator

text: You're wasting a spell.
speaker: narrator

text: There's nothing here to open.
speaker: narrator

text: The Ogre's chest is already unlocked.
speaker: narrator

text: Your spell unlocks the Ogre's chest.
speaker: narrator

text: Your spell is too weak to open the Ogre's chest.
speaker: narrator

text: The chest is held shut by the Ogre's massive arms.
speaker: narrator

text: You want to fetch THAT?
speaker: narrator

text: The dead Ogre's huge club is much too heavy for you to lift.
speaker: narrator

text: You need to get closer to the dead Ogre.
speaker: narrator

text: A search of the dead Ogre's body produces no treasure.
speaker: narrator

text: However, the treasure chest he was carrying lies beside his body.
speaker: narrator

text: The lock on the Ogre's chest clicks open.
speaker: narrator

text: The dead Ogre's chest contains 1 gold and 43 silver, which you take and put away.
speaker: narrator

text: You force the Ogre's chest open.
speaker: narrator

text: The chest is locked.
speaker: narrator

text: The dead Ogre's chest is empty.
speaker: narrator

text: The Ogre appears to have recovered from your spell.
speaker: narrator

text: They're like stalagmites, but they go the other way.
speaker: narrator

text: They're like stalactites, but they go the other way.
speaker: narrator

text: The cavern contains some impressive formations and is rather beautiful, as caves go.
speaker: narrator

text: The stalactites, stalagmites, and cave walls glow from a phosphorescent fungus growing there.
speaker: narrator
